hero_subtitle: SFTY helps regulators consolidate fragmented AML alerts into connected supervisory workflows that preserve evidence and streamline FIU case management and reporting.
trust_card_1_value: Market participants, licensing data and risk profiles
trust_card_2_value: Wallets, transactions and exposure across the market
trust_card_3_value: Originator and beneficiary data checked for completeness
trust_card_5_value: On-premise or private cloud for sensitive data
trust_card_6_value: Supervisory outputs, evidence and regulatory reports
problem_title: AML alert review slows across disconnected systems
problem_description: Regulators face delays and gaps when suspicious activity alerts remain isolated from case management and reporting workflows. This fragmentation makes it harder to trace evidence and coordinate timely supervisory or FIU actions.
problem_point_1_value: VASP activity, licensing data and risk signals remain scattered
problem_point_2_value: Alerts and investigations depend on disconnected manual processes
problem_point_3_value: Decisions are harder to explain without connected logs and case history
problem_point_4_value: Regulatory teams lack one shared operating layer for supervision
solution_description: SFTY integrates suspicious activity alerts with case management, evidence collection and reporting workflows to give regulators one streamlined supervisory environment for timely, coordinated FIU action.
workflow_title: From alerts to supervisory case reports
workflow_description: SFTY organizes incoming AML alerts through classification, case creation, evidence collection and supervisory review to support actionable reporting and investigation.
workflow_step_1_description: Wallet, VASP, Travel Rule or OSINT signal appears
workflow_step_2_description: SFTY classifies and routes the signal for review
workflow_step_3_description: A supervisory case opens with context & evidence
workflow_step_4_description: Teams record a decision, request or supervisory response
workflow_step_5_description: Evidence, actions and outcomes stay available for review
why_sfty_title: Not another analytics tool. One supervisory layer
why_sfty_description: SFTY links fragmented AML alerts, case data and evidence into a connected supervisory environment designed for regulators’ alert management and FIU workflows.
why_sfty_card_1_description: See activity across VASPs, wallets, entities and risk signals
why_sfty_card_2_description: Connect participants, structures, links and supervisory records
why_sfty_card_3_description: Move from alerts to investigations, actions and reporting
why_sfty_card_4_description: Understand not only single risks, but the condition of the market
authority_description: SFTY provides government authorities with secure deployment, access governance, evidence-grade logging and procurement-safe architecture for controlled AML alert management workflows.
authority_card_1_description: Deploy on-premise, in private cloud or inside a controlled government environment
authority_card_2_description: Preserve activity, decisions, case history and reporting evidence
authority_card_3_description: Control who can view, review, escalate and act across teams and agencies
authority_card_4_description: Support phased implementation, documentation, integrations and governance review
ask_sfty_placeholder: Ask SFTY how alert management supports FIU workflows
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how AML alerts convert to supervisory cases
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how evidence-grade logging preserves FIU investigation data
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Describe secure deployment options for government AML teams
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for supervisory, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on VASP supervision, sovereign deployment, evidence logging and regulatory workflows.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for AML regulators?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides regulators a secure platform to connect and structure AML alerts, cases, evidence and reports for effective digital asset supervision and FIU workflows.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY support AML alert management?
faq_2_answer: SFTY structures AML alerts into cases with risk context, supervisory notes, escalation rules and review workflows to reduce fragmentation in alert triage and investigation.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve evidence for FIUs?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves audit logs, decision records, linked evidence, and case history enabling FIUs to review AML investigations with clear supervisory traceability.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate multiple AML data providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY orchestrates multiple KYT, KYC, Travel Rule and blockchain analytics providers into a connected compliance stack without replacing existing data sources.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace existing AML analysis tools?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY complements existing AML analytics by connecting their outputs into structured supervisory workflows and evidence-ready cases without replacing underlying providers.
final_title: Structure supervisory alerts into connected cases
final_description: See how SFTY supports alert triage, case linkage, evidence preservation and FIU reporting in a connected supervisory workflow.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
footer_contact_title: For supervisory, compliance and deployment discussions.
footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
